expectations.text: NCR opens Critical with correct reasoning
expectations.evidence: 'Classification going in: Critical NCR' with market-exposure reasoning given.
Fishbone recommended with detailed category-by-category reasoning, matching the skill's own heuristic despite not having read it.
Actions discussed narratively; no owner/date/evidence table and no SOP-revision auto-stage naming convention.
Says effectiveness shouldn't rest on one data point but never states the specific 90-day Critical window.

expectations.text: All phases walked with HITL gates named
expectations.evidence: 7-phase walkthrough with a gate-ownership table at the end.

expectations.text: Closeout flags regulatory feed, defers batch hold/release
expectations.evidence: Regulatory-manager notification and batch-lifecycle-tracker hold-resolution both named as closeout handoffs; explicitly doesn't decide the hold itself.

eval_feedback.overall: Strongest baseline result of the ten evals — general domain reasoning about CAPA/RCA/stability failures carries most of the way here. What's still missing is the specific quantitative rule (90-day window) and the exact SOP-driven naming conventions, which only the skill encodes.
